title: 'Money means nothing — do what you love' — Sept 2019 aphorism
html:
TL;DR

  • Title-only Apple Note from Sept 17, 2019, 11:16pm — Yahir is 17, senior year at Grants Pass High. Title is the entire artifact: "(Money means nothing). Do what you love and want to do..." Body is empty. 37-second create→modify gap.
  • Pure manifesto style note aphorism, . The parenthetical "(Money means nothing)" + the trailing ellipsis are doing all the work — Yahir hedging the maximalism with a "well, sort of" gesture and then trailing off as if the rest is too obvious to write.
  • Anchors the Sept-Oct 2019 aphorism cluster alongside the "26 minute nap" capture and "Coding zeal" — Yahir at 17 oscillating between life-philosophy declarations, micro-self-quantification, and skill-area commitments inside a single five-week window.
  • The thesis is the vintage teen-Yahir version of what later evolves into the excessively net positive person / financial freedom enables what matters frame — same instinct, opposite emphasis (17-year-old Yahir denies money's relevance; 23-year-old Yahir treats financial freedom as the prerequisite for the "do what you love" part).
  • The aphorism reads against the Sept-2019 reality that Yahir was already prepping the Ford / Al Forthan scholarship pipelines — needing the money to attend OSU while writing notes claiming money means nothing. The contradiction is the formative tension.

Source

"(Money means nothing). Do what you love and want to do..."

Why it matters in the graph

  • Earliest explicit money-is-not-the-point statement in the corpus — the foundational counterweight that the entire bridge income / Rankett arc later argues with, not against.
  • Sets the dialectic: pre-OSU Yahir's "money means nothing" vs post-Austin Yahir's "$5k/mo by June 2026" — same person, four years and one financial-reality apart.

Open questions

  • Provoked by what? A specific Sept-2019 conversation, a Maxwell passage, a film? No context in note.
